subject: Civics (Social and Political Life - II)
chapter: Growing Up as Boys and Girls

activities_projects.title: Sociological Analysis of Samoan vs. Indian Patriarchy
activities_projects.objective: Deconstruct how social structures determine gender freedom
materials: Research papers or detailed summaries of Margaret Mead's study
steps: Compare the lack of 'adolescent distress' in 1920s Samoa with the restricted movement of girls in 1960s Madhya Pradesh
Analyze the 'point of transition' (when children moved from mother's care to community work) in both societies
Argue how the physical layout of a town (open vs. secluded) enforces gender norms
Evaluate why the Samoan model challenged the then-prevalent Western and Indian ideas of 'natural' gender behavior
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically assesses the 'Social Construction of Gender' versus biological determinism
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of sociological comparison and quality of the structural argument (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Economic Valuation of Unpaid Care Work (GDP Inclusion)
activities_projects.objective: Apply economic concepts to quantify devalued domestic labor
steps: Research the 'Time-Use Survey' results in India showing the hours women spend on 'unpaid care' vs. men
Using a 'Replacement Cost' method, calculate what a housewife's monthly salary would be based on minimum wages for various tasks
Discuss the concept of 'GDP' and why housework is currently excluded from it
Write a policy proposal on why recognizing this labor is essential for women's economic empowerment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Synthesizes economic principles with social justice to challenge the devaluing of domestic work
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calculations and logical strength of the policy proposal (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Invisible Labor' and Global Care Chains
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the intersection of gender, class, and domestic work
materials: Case study of Melani from the textbook
News articles on domestic worker rights
steps: Analyze Melani’s story to identify the 'triple burden' (poverty, caste/class, and gender)
Research the 'Domestic Workers Social Security Act' or similar local laws
Investigate why domestic work is often excluded from 'formal' labor protections
Create a report on the 'Global Care Chain'—how women from poorer regions migrate to do domestic work in richer areas
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Examines how gender inequality is compounded by economic and social status
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical analysis of labor laws and the intersectional nature of Melani’s struggle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constitutional Ethics: Equality in the Private Sphere
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the conflict between traditional roles and Constitutional mandates
materials: Legal case summaries on domestic rights
steps: The Constitution says being male or female should not be a reason for discrimination. Analyze if this holds true 'inside' the Indian home
Discuss the legal status of 'Housework'—is it a choice, a duty, or a form of forced labor?
Analyze why the law often hesitates to interfere in the 'private sphere' of the family
Draft a 'Home Constitution' that aligns family roles with national democratic values
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Evaluates the implementation of democratic principles within the family unit
activities_projects.evaluation: Legal and ethical depth of the 'Home Constitution' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Spatial Segregation and the 'Male Gaze'
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the impact of safety and surveillance on women's mobility
steps: Based on the MP school case study, map out 'safe' and 'unsafe' zones in your locality for girls
Identify 'liminal spaces' (streets, corners) where boys loiter and girls feel the need to move fast
Interview older women to see how their 'fear of the street' was taught as 'modesty'
Write a critique on how urban planning can either restrict or liberate gender movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyzes the relationship between physical space, safety, and gendered behavior
activities_projects.evaluation: Observation of spatial patterns and critical reflection on mobility (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Deconstructing the 'Double Burden' Syndrome
activities_projects.objective: Explore the psychological and physical impact of dual roles
steps: Define 'Double Burden' using qualitative data from three working women in different sectors
Analyze the 'Leisure Gap'—the difference in free time between men and women in the same household
Investigate the 'Second Shift'—a term for housework done after a full day of paid work
Propose a 'Shared Responsibility Model' that accounts for mental labor (planning meals, managing kids)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the mental and physical toll of the uneven distribution of domestic labor
activities_projects.evaluation: Insightfulness of the 'Leisure Gap' analysis and the model's viability (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critique of Government Intervention: The Crèche Law
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the effectiveness of legislative measures for equality
steps: Study the law requiring crèches in establishments with over 50 employees (updated from 30 in text)
Find out why many companies avoid hiring women to bypass these 'costly' legal requirements
Discuss if such laws 'unintentionally' lead to hiring discrimination
Debate: 'Should the cost of crèches be borne by the employer, or the State through taxes?'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Critically appraises the gaps between law-making and law-implementation regarding gender
activities_projects.evaluation: Complexity of the debate and understanding of labor market dynamics (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Media and the 'Care Economy' Narrative
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how advertisements devalue care-giving work
materials: TV advertisements for detergents, soaps, and insurance
steps: Watch 5 ads where a woman is shown 'happily' doing laundry or cooking for the family
Identify the 'glorification' of self-sacrifice—how the ad makes the woman's 'unpaid work' seem like a 'labor of love' only
Explain how this 'glorification' prevents people from seeing it as 'strenuous labor'
Redesign an ad that shows a man and woman sharing a domestic task as a professional responsibility
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the subtle ways media romanticizes domestic labor to avoid economic questioning
activities_projects.evaluation: Critical deconstruction of ad narratives and the quality of the redesign (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Pink Collar' Ghettoization Inquiry
activities_projects.objective: Link childhood socialization to professional segregation
materials: Employment statistics by gender and sector
steps: Research why certain jobs (Nursing, Teaching) are 'Pink Collar' and why they offer lower pay than 'Blue/White Collar' jobs
Connect this to the 'toys' and 'stories' given to girls (dolls, tea sets) as discussed in the book
Analyze the 'Glass Ceiling'—why women are concentrated at the 'care' level and absent from 'leadership' level
Explain how 'socializing' girls to be 'nurturers' limits their economic potential
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Correlates childhood gender roles with long-term economic outcomes and career limits
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity in connecting socialization with labor market trends (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sustainable Equality: A Policy Draft for Rural India
activities_projects.objective: Design interventions for specific socio-geographic contexts
materials: Information on Anganwadis and Self-Help Groups (SHGs)
steps: Identify the specific 'time-poverty' of rural women (fetching water, firewood, cattle care)
Draft a policy that uses technology (e.g., piped water, solar cookers) to reduce 'unpaid labor time'
Explain how 'Self-Help Groups' can help shift the 'devaluation' of women's work into 'income-generating' work
Argue why 'Equality at Home' is the first step toward 'Sustainable Development Goals' (SDG 5)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Integrates gender equality with sustainable development and technological intervention
activities_projects.evaluation: Feasibility and innovation of the rural policy draft (10 marks)
